label: Thyroid
quick_take: Thyroid is the most dramatic cancer-trend story in CI5, period. **South Korea** stands alone: registry-level female ASR around 120 per 100 000 in vol XI — nearly 10× the world median and higher than any other registry-sex-cancer combination in the dataset. This is the textbook case of screening-driven over-diagnosis and CI5 captures it with unmatched clarity.

findings.title: South Korea: Daegu women, vol XI, ASR 119.7. Gwangju 108.8. Daejeon 108.6. Ulsan 96.6. Seoul 93.8
findings.text: Vol XI (2008–2012) was the screening-bubble peak. By vol XII the rates had fallen back (Daegu 87.3, Seoul 64.7) as the over-screening was reined in. Inverted-U shapes in virtually every Korean city. This is the single cleanest public-health intervention effect in CI5.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — South Korea: Daegu women, vol XI, ASR 119.7. Gwangju 108.8. Daejeon 108.6. Ulsan 96.6. Seoul 93.8

findings.title: Chinese cities are rising fast — not yet past peak
findings.text: Jiashan County women +4432 % (1.18 → 53.48); Harbin Nangang +2665 %; Wuhan +1834 %; Qidong (U-shape) 0.69 → 12.19. Men in Jiashan +2875 %, Beijing +1888 %, Wuhan +1575 %. The Chinese story looks exactly like the Korean one did at vol X — rise still ongoing.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Chinese cities are rising fast — not yet past peak

findings.title: Italy Latina women: 1.55 → 31.83 peak → 29.66
findings.text: a European microcosm of the Korean pattern. Italy generally is a high-and-rising thyroid country.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Italy Latina women: 1.55 → 31.83 peak → 29.66

findings.title: Sex ratio: thyroid is one of the few cancers where women outweigh men 3–5 to 1 almost everywhere
findings.text: The strength of the imbalance is a thyroid-cancer fingerprint.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Sex ratio: thyroid is one of the few cancers where women outweigh men 3–5 to 1 almost everywhere

findings.title: Early peak (age band 6–7, ~25–35 yrs) in many populations with high screening uptake
findings.text: USA Iowa and Wisconsin women peak at 25–34, as do Trivandrum, several Chinese cities. This is papillary thyroid cancer being found incidentally in young women during other imaging.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Early peak (age band 6–7, ~25–35 yrs) in many populations with high screening uptake

findings.title: US ethnic contrasts: 15× between Asian-Pacific and Native American groups
findings.text: **US ethnic contrasts: 15× between Asian-Pacific and Native American groups.**
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — US ethnic contrasts: 15× between Asian-Pacific and Native American groups

findings.title: Hawaii Chinese women vol II: ASR 22.86, 7.6× the world median of 1970
findings.text: An early, pre-CT outlier that has not received much attention. Based on ~21 cases in the Hawaii-Chinese subset — level is credible but the trend should not be parsed from vol II alone.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Hawaii Chinese women vol II: ASR 22.86, 7.6× the world median of 1970

findings.title: Bimodal age curves in several Western registries
findings.text: (LA Non-Hispanic White, Detroit White, Israel Jews). Two-peak distributions: one young, one old — reflects the papillary-vs-follicular histology split.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Bimodal age curves in several Western registries

findings.text: Most of the rise globally is *incidentalomas* — sub-clinical papillary cancers detected during ultrasound for unrelated reasons. Mortality from thyroid cancer has barely moved. Be explicit in any writeup.
No formal CI5 cancer_warning but the histology mix (papillary / follicular / medullary / anaplastic) is not stated; the screening signal is overwhelmingly papillary.

findings.title: The Korean national-screening program's rise and fall is the single most striking epidemiological intervention signal in CI5
findings.text: A standalone chart of Daegu / Gwangju / Daejeon / Ulsan / Seoul inverted-U would be the strongest plot the project can produce.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — The Korean national-screening program's rise and fall is the single most striking epidemiological intervention signal in CI5

findings.title: China is on the same trajectory 5–10 years behind Korea
findings.text: a prospective comparison could warn policy makers that the Chinese inverted-U peak is still ahead.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — China is on the same trajectory 5–10 years behind Korea

findings.text: The young-age peak (25–35) for papillary thyroid cancer is globally consistent; documenting the age-shift across vols I–XII tells the over-diagnosis story chronologically.
